symbols: trident (trishula) — attribute of Shiva representing creation, preservation, and destruction
crescent moon — Shiva as Chandrashekhara, the lord who bears the moon in his hair
snow-capped mountains — Mount Kailash, the mythological abode of Shiva
sacred cow (Nandi) — Shiva's divine vehicle and symbol of dharma
third eye on forehead — Shiva's eye of wisdom and destruction of illusion
lotus flowers — purity and divine beauty in Hindu iconography
description: A surrealist composite portrait in which a large female face is formed by the arrangement of smaller figures and elements. At the center-top, a blue-skinned four-armed deity sits in meditation within the woman's forehead area. The woman's eyes are formed by two smaller human figures in reclining poses. A trident rises on the left side. Snow-covered mountains fill the background. A crescent moon appears in the upper right sky. A white bull stands at the lower left. Lotus flowers are visible near the lower portion. The palette features rich golds, deep blues, warm reds, and cool mountain whites.
